fn1: The numbering of chapters is sequential throughout the translation, instead of separate numbering of chapters within sections. This is also the pattern followed in the critical edition.
fn5: The word means to be deprived of, or perishing.
fn7: Maintenance of the special ﬁre (Agni) and the offering of oblations to it.
fn9: Sacriﬁces on the day of the new moon.
fn10: Sacriﬁces on the day of the full moon.
fn11: There were originally thirty-three gods—eleven in heaven, eleven in the sky and eleven on earth. Another way of getting the number is by adding twelve adityas, eleven rudras and ten vishvadevas.
fn12: Exclamations made at the time of offering oblations.
fn14: Exclamations made at the time of offering oblations.
fn16: An apsara. Apsaras are celestial maidens associated with Indra’s court. They are sometimes regarded as wives of gandharvas.
fn22: A snake that is not poisonous.
